channel.title: OKIMesh on OKI Mesh
channel.description: Recent content in OKIMesh on OKI Mesh

item.title: Coverage
item.description:
Coverage grows every week as members put up nodes. The map below shows current repeaters and known coverage across Greater Cincinnati, Dayton, and Northern Kentucky.

Want to fill a gap? High spots — water towers, ridgelines, tall rooftops — make the biggest difference. Reach out if you can host a repeater.

item.title: Getting Started
item.description:
New to mesh? Start here. You’ll have a node on the air in an afternoon.

1. Get a radio

Almost any MeshCore-compatible LoRa board works. Good first nodes for the 915 MHz US band:

  • Heltec T114 - cheap, easy to flash, low power, great for a first client node.
  • Heltec V3 - cheap, easy to flash, great for a first client node.
  • RAK WisBlock (RAK4631) - low power, ideal for solar repeaters.
  • Seeed T1000-E - pocketable tracker-style client.

2. Flash MeshCore

Flash the latest MeshCore firmware for your board, then set the regional parameters so you land on the OKI Mesh:
